generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PermanentMagnetMotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted on the permanent magnet synchronous motor measuring output angle with 0.06 mm repeatability, streamed via MQTT for real-time transmission to the digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance GearTransmissionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder positioned behind the cycloidal gear transmission measuring output angle with 0.06 mm repeatability, streamed via MQTT for real-time transmission to the digital representation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input current of the motor, integrated with the permanent magnet synchronous motor drive system, streamed via MQTT for real-time transmission."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PermanentMagnetMotorEncoder
]

instance GearEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearTransmissionEncoder
]

instance CurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance MotionAccuracyModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based Internal Block Diagram representing the functional architecture of the MABI MAX 100 robot arm motion control system for precision manufacturing tasks."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance FrictionAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink-based dynamic simulation model capturing gear transmission friction characteristics for predictive maintenance analysis in machining operations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance OperationalTraceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces including position encoder readings, current measurements, and operational logs for process optimization analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, GearEncoderDataTransmitted, CurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance ProcessOptimizationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived data representing higher-level abstractions from raw sensor data including motion accuracy metrics, tool wear indicators, and performance trends."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalTraceData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictionEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (Systems Modeling Language) platform enabling functional and logical architecture modeling of the robot controller and machining system components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionAccuracyVisualizationService, ProcessOptimizationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation engine for detailed behavioral modeling of gear transmission dynamics and motion control systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService, SimulationValidationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Contextual data aggregation system implementing MQTT-based streaming and preprocessing pipelines for sensor data normalization and validation."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringDashboardService
]

instance PredictionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI/ML integration framework (implied) for predictive analytics on processed operational data to identify deviations in motion accuracy and tool performance."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance MQTTDataStreamingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based publish-subscribe protocol implementation for real-time transmission of sensor data from physical components to digital twin processing components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance MotionAccuracyVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard service visualizing motion accuracy metrics and position deviations through SysML-based Internal Block Diagrams for stakeholder monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyInsight
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-driven optimization service using SysML models to validate machining parameter changes before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

instance MonitoringDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Aggregated data visualization service presenting operational metrics, tool wear indicators, and performance trends through data-driven dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStatusInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation service analyzing gear transmission friction characteristics using Simulink models for predictive maintenance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance SimulationValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model validation service comparing simulated behavior against real-time operational data to ensure digital twin fidelity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationConfidenceInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics service identifying potential deviations in motion accuracy or tool performance through processed operational data analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceAlertAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance MotionAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time motion accuracy metrics derived from encoder data comparisons showing deviations from target positions in machining operations."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Process optimization suggestions generated through simulation-driven analysis of machining parameters for improved precision and efficiency."
]

instance OperationalStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated operational status information including tool wear indicators, system health metrics, and performance trends for decision support."
]

instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed analysis of gear transmission friction characteristics identifying potential sources of motion inaccuracies in the machining system."
]

instance ValidationConfidenceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confidence measures comparing simulated behavior against real-time operational data to validate digital twin accuracy."
]

instance MaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proactive maintenance alerts generated from predictive analytics indicating potential tool wear or motion accuracy deviations requiring operator intervention."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Physical System Components
instance SINUMERIK840DController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller providing motion control and automation for the MABI MAX 100 machining system in precision manufacturing."
]

instance MABIMax100RobotArm : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "MABI MAX 100 collaborative robot designed for machining applications with high-precision motion control requirements."
]

instance CycloidalGearTransmission : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Gear transmission system incorporating cycloidal gears for precise motion conversion between motor and robot arm in the machining system."
]

// C2: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industry 4.0 manufacturing environment supporting continuous process optimization and real-time motion accuracy monitoring."
    base:contains SINUMERIK840DController, MABIMax100RobotArm, CycloidalGearTransmission, PermanentMagnetMotorEncoder, GearTransmissionEncoder, CurrentSensor
]

// C1: System Under Study
instance MABIMAX100System : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Digital Twin system for MABI MAX 100 machining robot with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D controller."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment ManufacturingEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents for Digital Twins. However, the distinction between **digital shadows** (passive, one-way data flow) and **Digital Twins** (active, bidirectional) [32] implies that DTs may include:
- **Control Flows**: Hypothetical **command types** (e.g., parameter adjustments, maintenance triggers) sent from the digital model to physical systems, though no specific formats or protocols are described.
- **Validation/Authentication**: Not mentioned; security mechanisms (e.g., OAuth, TLS) are implied but unspecified.
- **Feedback Systems**: No explicit feedback loops (e.g., closed-loop control) are referenced in the documents.
- **Conflict Resolution**: Not addressed; potential conflicts between digital and physical states are not discussed.
- **Command Execution Confirmation**: Not detailed; no mention of acknowledgment mechanisms or retry policies.
**Conclusion**: This characteristic is **Not in Document** for the described system.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** through structured data trace aggregation and abstraction, where **data traces** (collected from physical systems) are processed with **latency-sensitive computational steps** within the Processing component. The reference model supports **fine-grained traceability** of data traces back to their originating processing components, ensuring temporal alignment between physical and digital states. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms) or latency requirements are not quantified, the system relies on **real-time or near-real-time data flows** (implied by the one-way data aggregation in digital shadows) and **transitive traceability** to maintain temporal consistency. The use of **simulation and data calculation models** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, other simulation tools) further enables **time-scale abstraction** for higher-level computations, though granularity specifics (e.g., microsecond vs. second-level updates) are not detailed in the provided context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s **orchestration and coordination** are structured around a **reference model-based approach**, where **conforming models** (e.g., Class Diagrams) incarnate abstract concepts into concrete elements. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** defines a **processing-centric architecture** with:
   - **Processing components** (encoding logic via data calculation or simulation models),
   - **Data traces** (aggregated from physical systems),
   - **Metadata** (supporting further processing),
   - **Traceability links** (associations between traces, components, and models).
   Orchestration relies on **methodological concretization** of reference models (e.g., mapping reference concepts to use-case-specific elements) and **fine-grained traceability** to ensure system-wide consistency. The system does not explicitly mention **architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) or **resource management strategies**, but the **modular, purpose-driven design** implies **adaptive coordination** via conformance relations and hierarchical traceability. Integration of components is implied through **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, general-purpose languages) and **data-driven workflows**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system, as implied by the provided documents, is structured through a **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** framework, which includes distinct abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These layers are managed via methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which guide the transition between conceptual and implementation phases. The lifecycle governance involves **conformance relations** between reference models (e.g., Digital Shadow Reference Model) and concrete realizations, ensuring traceability via **UML Class Diagrams (CDs)** and **association mappings** that link data traces to processing components. The lifecycle also incorporates **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** to align modeling notations (e.g., SysML, AADL, MontiArc) and tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java) across phases. Transition management is implicit in the **purpose-driven concretization** of reference models, where designers adapt abstract concepts (e.g., data traces, processing logic) to domain-specific use cases (e.g., injection molding) while maintaining **fine-grained traceability** through transitive associations of data traces to models and components."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **fidelity** through the integration of **white-box (physics-based) and black-box (data-driven) models**, ensuring comprehensive representation of nonlinear phenomena (e.g., robot friction). **Validation** is achieved via **deviation analysis between simulated and measured data**, as referenced in Section IV.A. **Traceability** is emphasized in model-based development to verify correctness of transformations (e.g., from SysML to simulation models). However, explicit details on **uncertainty quantification, error detection mechanisms, or quality assurance processes** are not provided in the documents. The system relies on **probabilistic latent variable models** (implied in broader research but not explicitly cited here) and **adaptive control methods** for reliability, though these are not detailed in the given context."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this system is primarily defined by the **MQTT protocol**, which facilitates the **one-way streaming of sensor data** from physical objects to their digital shadow. No additional details regarding network topology (e.g., hybrid, star, mesh), bandwidth requirements, latency specifications, or redundancy mechanisms are provided. Security protocols or encryption methods (e.g., TLS) are not mentioned. The system’s communication is unidirectional, aligning with the **digital shadow reference model** as a passive data receiver."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The system leverages **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, utilizing **SysML** for architecture modeling and **MathWorks Simulink** for executable simulation models to represent system behavior. The deployment infrastructure is not explicitly detailed, but the use of **MBSE tools** implies a **model-driven approach** for development and execution. No specific hosting platforms (cloud, edge, or on-premise) are mentioned, nor are computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models (e.g., containerized, virtualized) addressed. The focus remains on **modeling and simulation tools** rather than deployment specifics."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems primarily through **MQTT for live data streaming** and **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for standardized asset representation, as explicitly mentioned in the documents. No other industrial data exchange protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST, SOAP, ESBs) are referenced. The integration follows a **purpose-driven data flow**, where external data (e.g., from IoT sensors, enterprise systems) is ingested as **data traces** and processed by the Digital Twin’s Processing Components. The absence of explicit mention of RESTful APIs, SOAP services, or enterprise service buses (ESBs) indicates that integration is **protocol-specific to MQTT and AAS**, with no broader interoperability frameworks described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI robot incorporates a **safety cell** restriction to ensure operational safety, limiting the maximum working range to **[-90°, 90°]**, as explicitly documented in the context of friction modeling and robot dynamics (Trinh et al., 2023). While the system streams data via **MQTT** (Light, 2017), no explicit security protocols (e.g., TLS, OAuth, or role-based access control) are described. The Digital Shadow framework (Heithoff et al., 2025) emphasizes **motion accuracy** and **understanding of physical behavior** but does not detail fail-safe mechanisms, threat mitigation, or risk assessment methodologies. The provided documents do not reference **RAMI 4.0** or **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** security standards, nor do they mention authentication systems, consent management, or data anonymization techniques. The only safety-related specification is the **geometric confinement** of the robot's operational envelope, which serves as a basic safety measure."
]
